steps.title: Use the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content: The two given lengths are the legs of a right triangle, so the hypotenuse comes from $c^2=24^2+21^2$. Factor the result to match the form $3\sqrt{d}$.

steps.title: Factor the legs and apply the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
Notice that both legs have a factor of 3: $24 = 3(8)$ and $21 = 3(7)$. Using the Pythagorean theorem:
$$c^2 = 24^2 + 21^2 = [3(8)]^2 + [3(7)]^2$$

steps.title: Simplify to find d
steps.content:
Expand the squared terms:
$$c^2 = 9(64) + 9(49) = 9(64 + 49) = 9(113)$$
Taking the square root:
$$c = \sqrt{9(113)} = 3\sqrt{113}$$
Comparing to $3\sqrt{d}$, we have $d = 113$.
